verses.english: This entire universe is pervaded by you and interwoven in you. You are pure consciousness by nature. Do not settle for a petty, limited state of mind.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra uses the classic image of thread running through fabric. The universe is not separate from you; it is made out of your own conscious substance. He explicitly orders Janaka to drop *kṣudracittatām*—the small, insecure mindset of a fragile human ego.

understand.common_misconception: Feeling tiny, isolated, and permanently anxious inside a massive, cold, and uncaring cosmic map.
understand.attainment_meaning: Recognizing that the entire universe arises and takes its shape inside your wide-open consciousness.

verses.philosophy: Awareness is the fundamental fabric of reality (*Vibhva*). Adopting a fragile, separate identity contradicts the boundless nature of the true Self.

verses.sanskrit:
निरपेक्षो निर्विकारो निर्भरः शीतलाशयः ।
अगाधबुद्धिसम्पन्नो भव चिन्मात्रवासना ॥
verses.transliteration:
nirapēkṣō nirvikārō nirbharaḥ śītalāśayaḥ |
agādhabuddhisampannō bhava cinmātravāsanā ||
verses.english: You are independent, unchanging, absolute, and serene. You are endowed with profound intelligence. Remain centered solely in pure awareness.
verses.commentary: True peace means having a completely cool mind (*śītalāśayaḥ*), untouched by hot desires or reactive emotional swings. When the mind stops seeking external validation, it settles into its natural state.

understand.common_misconception: Fearing that spiritual independence will leave you cold, indifferent, or completely blank.
understand.attainment_meaning: It brings a stable, deep peace that allows you to engage with life without being thrown off balance by it.

verses.philosophy: When attention shifts away from worldly modifications and rests completely on the background of awareness (*Chinmatra*), old mental habits dissolve naturally.

verses.sanskrit:
साकारमनृतं विद्धि निराकारं तु निश्चलम् ।
एतत्तत्त्वोपदेशेन न पुनर्भवसम्भवः ॥
verses.transliteration:
sākāramanṛtaṃ viddhi nirākāraṃ tu niścalam |
etattattvopadeśena na punarbhavasambhavaḥ ||
verses.english: Know that what has form is temporary and insubstantial, and only the formless is eternal. Through this direct instruction, you will not fall back into the illusion of birth and death.
verses.commentary: Forms are constantly morphing and changing, which means they lack absolute reality. Only the formless space behind them remains completely steady. Changing your focus from the scene to the witness destroys the fear of mortality.

understand.common_misconception: Thinking that calling the physical world 'false' means it doesn't exist at all or should be hated.
understand.attainment_meaning: It means the world is temporary and shifting, like a movie on a screen, whereas your awareness is the solid screen underneath.

verses.philosophy: This is the core distinction between *Drisya* (the changing scene) and *Drashta* (the unchanging witness), which dissolves the illusion of cyclical identity (*Punarbhava*).

verses.sanskrit:
यथैव आदर्शमध्यस्थे रूपे अन्तः परितस्तु सः ।
तथैव अस्मिन् शरीरे अन्तः परितः परमेश्वरः ॥
verses.transliteration:
yathaiva ādarśamadhyasthe rūpe antaḥ paritastu saḥ |
tathaiva asmin śarīre antaḥ paritaḥ parameśvaraḥ ||
verses.english: Just as a mirror exists both inside and outside the reflection it shows, the Supreme Self exists both inside and outside this physical body.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra shares a beautiful mirror metaphor. The images on the glass don't divide, scratch, or alter the quality of the mirror itself; it contains them completely. Your body is a temporary reflection occurring inside the mirror of your boundless consciousness.

understand.common_misconception: Believing that your soul is a small, localized spark locked up inside your heart or brain.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing that your body is moving inside your awareness, rather than your awareness being trapped inside your body.

verses.philosophy: Awareness is non-local and continuous (*Parameśvara*). It exists both inside and outside physical forms, rendering boundaries completely illusory.

verses.sanskrit:
एकं सर्वगतं व्योम बहिरन्तर्यथा घटे ।
नित्यं निरन्तरं ब्रह्म सर्वभूतगणे तथा ॥
verses.transliteration:
ekaṃ sarvagataṃ vyoma bahirantaryathā ghaṭe |
nityam nirantaraṃ brahma sarvabhūtagaṇe tathā ||
verses.english: Just as the same space exists inside and outside a clay pot, the eternal, all-pervading Brahman exists continuously within all beings.
verses.commentary: The classic pot-space metaphor (*Ghatakasha*). Breaking the clay pot doesn't shatter, move, or destroy the space inside it; it simply reveals that the inner and outer space were always one. The drop of the body is just like the breaking of a clay pot.

understand.common_misconception: Worrying that when your physical body dies, your personal consciousness will be destroyed along with it.
understand.attainment_meaning: The physical shell might break, but the space inside remains exactly as it always was.

verses.philosophy: Individualized consciousness (*Jivatman*) and universal consciousness (*Paramatman*) are fundamentally identical. The apparent boundary is temporary and nominal.

verses.sanskrit:
आत्मा साक्षी विभुः पूर्णः एको मुक्तश्चिदाशयः ।
अक्रियो निःसङ्गः स्पृहाशान्तो भ्रमवाद्विव ॥
verses.transliteration:
ātmā sākṣī vibhuḥ pūrṇaḥ eko muktaści dāśayaḥ |
akriyo niḥsaṅgaḥ spṛhāśānto bhramādvia iva ||
verses.english: The true Self is the all-pervading, perfect, solitary witness. It is free, conscious, actionless, unattached, desireless, and quiet. Through illusion alone does it seem involved in the world.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra lists the definitive checklist of your true identity. The Self isn't evolving toward perfection; it is already the unchanging foundation of experience, observing life without needing things to complete it.

understand.common_misconception: Viewing the Self as something incomplete that needs to collect experiences to grow.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing your core identity is already whole, observing life without needing things to complete it.

verses.philosophy: The nature of the absolute is inherently complete (*Purna*). It does not undergo transitions, modifications, or spiritual progress.

verses.sanskrit:
कूटस्थं बोधमद्वैतमात्मानं परिभावय ।
आभासोऽहं भ्रमं मुक्त्वा भावं बाह्यमथान्तरम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
kūṭasthaṃ bodhamadvaitamātmānaṃ paribhāvaya |
ābhāso'haṃ bhramaṃ muktvā bhāvaṃ bāhyamathāntaram ||
verses.english: Meditate on the unchanging, non-dual Self. Drop the illusion of being an isolated reflection, and leave behind both internal and external attachments.
verses.commentary: The term *Kutastha* refers to an anvil, which remains completely unmoved despite being struck by countless hammers. The Self is that solid foundation. Stepping back from the ego entirely reveals it is just a passing reflection.

understand.common_misconception: Believing your spiritual journey is about repairing your human ego or personality structure.
understand.attainment_meaning: Stepping back from the ego entirely, realizing it is just a passing reflection on the surface of your awareness.

verses.philosophy: The individual personality (*Jiva*) is a localized reflection of pure awareness. Freedom means shifting your identity from the reflection to the source.

verses.sanskrit:
देहाभिमानपाशेन बद्धोऽसि तात सर्वदा ।
बोधोऽहं ज्ञानखङ्गेन तन्निकृत्य सुखी भव ॥
verses.transliteration:
dehābhimānapāśena baddho'si tāta sarvada |
bodho'haṃ jñānakhaṅgena tannikṛtya sukhī bhava ||
verses.english: My child, you have long been bound by the rope of body-identification. Cut through that bond with the sword of knowledge—'I am pure awareness'—and be happy.
verses.commentary: Body-identification functions like a tight cord around your awareness. Ashtavakra points to sharp discrimination (*Jnana*) to cut it clean away. Using clear insight to see through the illusion immediately cuts the attachment without an uphill struggle.

understand.common_misconception: Assuming that untying this bond requires slow, painful effort or physical discipline.
understand.attainment_meaning: Using clear insight to see through the illusion immediately, which cuts the attachment without an uphill struggle.

verses.philosophy: The root cause of human suffering (*Samsara*) is *Deha-Abhimana* (body-ownership). Shifting identity to awareness brings immediate release.

verses.sanskrit:
न साक्षो वर्तते लोको न साक्षी कस्यचित् तथा ।
स्वप्रकाशोऽसि निर्मुक्तो मा मतिं कुरु संसृतौ ॥
verses.transliteration:
na sākṣō vartatē lōkō na sākṣī kasyacit tathā |
savaprakāśō'si nirmuktō mā matiṃ kuru saṃsṛtau ||
verses.english: The world does not possess the witness, nor does the witness belong to the world. You are self-luminous and eternally free. Do not scatter your mind back into worldly anxiety.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra draws a clean line. Awareness hosts the world, but it doesn't merge with it. Just like a television screen displays a fire without actually catching fire, your consciousness displays worldly problems without being damaged by them.

understand.common_misconception: Worrying that your inner peace will be permanently broken if your external conditions become chaotic.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing that your core awareness sits cleanly outside the scene, completely immune to worldly disruptions.

verses.philosophy: This is the absolute freedom of the subject (*Drashta-Autonomy*). The witness is *Sva-Prakasha* (self-luminous) and completely detached from the objects it illuminates.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्य धीः कुरुते बुद्धिं सा बुद्धिस्तस्य बन्धिनी ।
न बुद्ध्या लिप्यते साक्षी स साक्षी सर्वदा सुखी ॥
verses.transliteration:
yasya dhīḥ kurutē buddhiṃ sā buddhistasya bandhinī |
na buddhyā lipyatē sākṣī sa sākṣī sarvadā sukhī ||
verses.english: If the mind tries to rationalize and capture awareness, that very rationalization becomes a cage. The witness cannot be stained by the intellect; it remains eternally happy.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra warns against the trap of excessive overthinking. You cannot figure out your way into awareness using intellectual data alone, because your intellect is an object being observed by awareness. Trying to capture the witness using thoughts is like trying to grab your own shadow.

understand.common_misconception: Treating spiritual growth like an intense academic exam where you must continuously argue and analyze theories.
understand.attainment_meaning: Dropping the intellectual arguments and resting simply as the quiet presence that is already available.

verses.philosophy: The witness is *Atendriya*—beyond the reach of the mind and intellect. True peace is realized when the intellect stops trying to manage reality and dissolves into the source.

verses.sanskrit:
यत्र तत्र स्थिते योगी न विशेषोऽस्ति कस्यचित् ।
अगाधबुद्धिसम्पन्नः शीतलाशय एव सः ॥
verses.transliteration:
yatra tatra sthitē yōgī na viśēṣō'sti kasyacit |
agādhabuddhisampannaḥ śītalāśaya ēva saḥ ||
verses.english: Wherever an awakened soul finds themselves, they remain completely balanced. Endowed with profound intelligence, they inhabit a cool, serene mind in every situation.
verses.commentary: An integrated sage doesn't need a special environment to stay clear. Whether they are sitting in a busy palace or walking through a quiet forest, their internal climate stays perfectly cool (*śītalāśaya*). They have stopped setting conditions for their peace.

understand.common_misconception: Assuming that you can only maintain a spiritual state if you live in a perfectly silent ashram away from real life.
understand.attainment_meaning: Living authentically and calmly in the middle of everyday reality, because your peace is driven from the inside out.

verses.philosophy: This is the integration of *Sahaja Sthiti*. The sage is entirely independent of spatial variables because they recognize that all spaces exist inside their own awareness.

verses.sanskrit:
न जानाति महायोगी लोकव्यवहारं किमपि वै ।
आत्मन्येव सदा रमते नित्यतृप्तः सुखी भव ॥
verses.transliteration:
na jānāti mahāyōgī lōkavyavahāraṃ kimapi vai |
ātmanyēva sadā ramatē nityatṛptaḥ sukhī bhava ||
verses.english: The great soul pays no psychological attention to worldly gossip or games. Finding continuous delight within their own nature, they remain eternally satisfied.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra notes that a sage completely ignores the transactional drama of worldly gossip, comparison, and ranking systems (*lōkavyavahāraṃ*). They don't waste energy calculating what people think of them because they are already full within themselves.

understand.common_misconception: Living in continuous stress over social status, trending opinions, and trying to please everyone around you.
understand.attainment_meaning: Stepping out of the comparison loop entirely, finding your baseline validation in your own presence.

verses.philosophy: This is *Atmarati*—delighting exclusively in the Self. When the mind ceases to seek fulfillment in passing external data, it achieves *Nitya-Tripti* (eternal satisfaction).

verses.sanskrit:
क्व गतं क्व च वा प्राप्तं क्व मोक्षः क्व च बन्धनम् ।
परमात्मनि निष्कम्पः स्वमहिम्नि स्थितोऽस्म्यहम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
kva gataṃ kva ca vā prāptaṃ kva mōkṣaḥ kva ca bandhanam |
paramātmani niṣkampaḥ svamahimni sthitō'smyaham ||
verses.english: Where is any loss, where is any gain, where is liberation, and where is bondage? Unmoving in supreme reality, I rest securely in my own inherent majesty.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra provides the exact internal script of a free being. Paths, progress, debits, and credits are relative concepts that belong to the mind's grid. Resting as the absolute witness, the entire dualistic structure of chasing after freedom collapses into perfect stillness.

understand.common_misconception: Treating your life as an endless spiritual marathon where you are constantly running to fix a broken soul.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing you are already at the finish line, which allows you to stop running and finally be present.

verses.philosophy: This lands on *Svamahima-Sthiti*—resting in your own inherent majesty. From the absolute standpoint, no movement or transformation ever occurred; awareness has always been home.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रकृत्या जायते विश्वं न मयि स्थितिः यथार्थतः ।
अक्रियोऽहं विमुक्तोऽहं चिन्मात्रोऽहं निरञ्जनः ॥
verses.transliteration:
prakṛtyā jāyatē viśvaṃ na mayi sthitiḥ yathārthataḥ |
akriyō'haṃ vimuktō'haṃ cinmātrō'haṃ nirañjanaḥ ||
verses.english: The universe unfolds naturally according to the laws of nature; it has no absolute footing in me. Actionless, free, pure consciousness, and stainless am I.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra clarifies the mechanics of existence. The physical universe moves, grows, and changes according to natural cosmic laws (*Prakriti*). Awareness doesn't participate in this cosmic grinding machine; it is the clean, silent space that hosts the entire display.

understand.common_misconception: Feeling personally crushed, stressed, and broken by the natural physical transitions of the material world.
understand.attainment_meaning: Allowing nature to execute its cycles smoothly while you remain anchored as the untouched observer.

verses.philosophy: This is the ultimate division between *Prakriti* (nature) and *Purusha* (pure awareness). The universe is dependently real, while your core consciousness is absolute, stainless (*Nirañjana*), and ever-free.

verses.sanskrit:
शरीरं स्वर्गनरकौ बन्धमोक्षौ भयं तथा ।
कल्पनामात्रमेवैतत् किं मे कार्यं चिदात्मनः ॥
verses.transliteration:
śarīraṃ svarganarakau bandhamokṣau bhayaṃ tathā |
kalpanāmātramevaitat kiṃ me kāryaṃ cidātmanaḥ ||
verses.english: The body, heaven and hell, bondage and liberation, as well as fear—these are all mere projections of the mind. What have I to do with any of them, I who am pure consciousness?
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra closes this massive chapter by completely clearing away all conceptual frameworks. Social rankings, religious fears of hell, and spiritual desires for heaven are recognized as mental imagery. Anchored as pure consciousness, you can drop all psychological baggage and rest in perfect, natural freedom.

understand.common_misconception: Believing that finding deep peace requires you to conform to rigid rules, dogmas, and superstitious tracking systems.
understand.attainment_meaning: Stepping out of all mental traps entirely, knowing that your true nature is eternally free from both worldly and heavenly anxieties.

verses.philosophy: This is *Sarvatma-Bhava*—the realization of absolute non-dual unity. When the appearance of a separate world dissolves into the reality of pure consciousness, all relative concepts like bondage, rules, and freedom disappear, leaving only absolute, timeless presence.

title: Shloka Shatka
description: The Elimination of Intellectual and Scriptural Attachment
text: In this chapter, traditionally titled Shloka Shatka Prakaranam (The Plane of Special Instruction), the sage Ashtavakra speaks directly to the subtle traps of spiritual intellectualism and structural attachment. He explains that reading endless scriptures, talking about high philosophies, or collecting spiritual explanations will never bring liberation. True freedom is a radical act of letting go of the intellect, stopping the search, and directly resting in the silence of your own unconditioned presence.

verses.sanskrit:
अष्टावक्र उवाच -
आचक्ष्व शृणु वा तात नानाशास्त्राणि भूरिशः ।
तथापि न तव स्वास्थ्यं सर्वविस्मरणादृते ॥
verses.transliteration:
aṣṭāvakra uvāca -
ācakṣva śṛṇu vā tāta nānāśāstrāṇi bhūriśaḥ |
tathāpi na tava svāsthyaṃ sarvavismaraṇādṛte ||
verses.english: Ashtavakra said: My dear child, you may quote scriptures, lecture on philosophies, or listen to endless explanations. Yet, you will never find pristine inner health until you completely forget everything in the silent space of your being.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra attacks the ultimate fortress of the spiritual ego: intellectual learning. You can memorize every book, win every debate, and use perfect spiritual jargon, but that information is just more weight in your mind. True clarity requires *sarvavismaraṇa*—the radical willingness to drop the conceptual maps and face reality raw.

understand.common_misconception: Believing that you can collect and study your way into enlightenment, treating it like an academic exam that requires massive data accumulation.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing that enlightenment is a process of unlearning and letting go of concepts, arriving at the quiet presence that exists before thoughts take shape.

verses.philosophy: Scriptural analysis (*Shastra-Vasana*) can become an obstacle to direct realization. Awareness is *Parodya*—beyond words. It is realized not by collecting definitions, but by entering the silent gap between them.

verses.sanskrit:
भोगं कर्म समाधिं वा कुरु विज्ञाय ते मनः ।
वस्तुस्थित्यात्मनोऽमुष्य विक्षिप्तं किमु जायते ॥
verses.transliteration:
bhogaṃ karma samādhiṃ vā kuru vijñāya te manaḥ |
vastusthityātmano'muṣya vikṣiptaṃ kimu jāyate ||
verses.english: Whether you engage in worldly enjoyment, perform intense actions, or force your mind into deep meditation—once you know the true nature of your presence, you realize it can never be distracted anyway.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra completely levels all conditional states. The mind believes it is 'good' when meditating and 'bad' when busy or enjoying something. Ashtavakra blows past this moral tracking. Since your core awareness is like wide-open space, it remains completely untouched, whether a storm of thoughts passes through or the sky stays perfectly clear.

understand.common_misconception: Living in constant fear of losing your spiritual state whenever your day gets chaotic, noisy, or active.
understand.attainment_meaning: Recognizing that your baseline awareness is always present and clean, hosting both noisy and quiet moments without being altered by them.

verses.philosophy: The Self is *Nitya-Shuddha* (eternally pure). Distraction (*Vikshepa*) and concentration (*Samadhi*) are properties of the mind-computer. Awareness stands cleanly outside these states, immune to their movement.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्याहंकारोऽस्ति स एव क्लिश्यते न तु कश्चिदन्यः ।
तस्मिन्मुक्ते सर्वं मुक्तं भवति सुखं चर ॥
verses.transliteration:
yasyāhaṃkāro'sti sa eva kliśyate na tu kaścidanyaḥ |
tasminmukte sarvaṃ muktaṃ bhavati sukhaṃ cara ||
verses.english: Only the one who carries the separate 'I' ego suffers; no one else. When that false center is released, everything is instantly liberated. Live happily.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra zeroes in on the single generator of human suffering: *Ahamkara* (the ego-sense). Situations themselves are neutral, but the moment the ego layers its personal claim onto a situation ('Why did this happen to *me*? What if *I* fail?'), suffering is instantly manufactured. Dissolve that false center, and life becomes light and clear.

understand.common_misconception: Blaming your stressful life, your family history, or your heavy schedule for your deep emotional exhaustion.
understand.attainment_meaning: Seeing that the exhaustion is caused entirely by the ego's frantic attempt to control and defend its identity, and dropping that performance brings immediate rest.

verses.philosophy: Suffering is completely non-situational; it is entirely ego-dependent. When the illusion of a separate personal agent drops, consciousness settles into its natural, open autonomy.

verses.sanskrit:
कस्य बन्धः क्व वा मोक्षो भ्रान्तिरेव हि कल्पिता ।
न बद्धो न च मुक्तोऽहं चिन्मात्रोऽहं निरञ्जनः ॥
verses.transliteration:
kasya bandhaḥ kva vā mokṣo bhrāntireva hi kalpitā |
na baddho na ca mukto'ahṃ cinmātro'haṃ nirañjanaḥ ||
verses.english: Whose bondage is it, and where is liberation? These are mere delusions projected by the mind. I am neither bound nor free; I am pure consciousness, ever stainless.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra takes the absolute standpoint. The very question 'How do I get free?' validates the initial mistake that you are currently trapped. By recognizing you are the continuous witness of all concepts, you realize you were never inside the cage to begin with. Both 'trapped' and 'free' are just labels passing through your presence.

understand.common_misconception: Treating your life as a long, arduous spiritual rescue mission where you must fix, heal, and rescue a permanently damaged soul.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing your core awareness has never been damaged, stained, or trapped by your personal history, which brings instant relief.

verses.philosophy: This is *Ajatavada* perspective showing up in practice. Duality exists only within the conceptual grid of the mind. Pure consciousness (*Cinmatra*) is continuous, undivided, and entirely beyond relative tracking.

verses.sanskrit:
यदा मनसि संकल्पः कश्चिद्वा संप्रजायते ।
तदैव बन्धनं विद्धि तन्नाशो मोक्ष उच्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
yadā manasi saṃkalpaḥ kaścidvā saṃprajāyate |
tadaiva bandanaṃ viddhi tannāśo mokṣa ucyate ||
verses.english: The exact millisecond a conceptual projection or desire takes root in the mind, know that very moment as bondage. The dissolution of that projection is what is called liberation.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra shows us how bondage happens in real time. Bondage is not a permanent, physical entity; it is a micro-movement of identification. The moment your mind creates a tight condition for your happiness ('I must get this outcome, or I am ruined'), it manufactures a state of lack. Dropping that calculation is liberation.

understand.common_misconception: Assuming that bondage is a massive, solid mountain of past karma that takes lifetimes of struggle to slowly chisel away.
understand.attainment_meaning: Seeing that bondage is just a habit of overthinking and clinging happening in the current moment, which can be dropped right now.

verses.philosophy: Bondage is dynamic and immediate, driven by *Sankalpa* (mental projection/intention). When the mind stops creating conditions and intentions, awareness naturally resets to its default, free state.

verses.sanskrit:
न किंचिच्चिन्तयेद् यस्तु स एव सुखी भवति ।
तस्मात्सर्वं परित्यज्य न किंचिदपि चिन्तयेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
na kiṃciccitayed yastu sa eva sukhī bhavati |
tasmātsarvaṃ parityajya na kiṃcidapi cintayet ||
verses.english: One who does not anxiously calculate or overthink anything becomes truly happy. Therefore, leaving behind all mental stories, do not anxiously project anything.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra calls for a complete halt to mental calculations (*na kiṃciccitayed*). He is not telling you to become brain-dead; he is telling you to stop the exhausting, endless self-commentary that over-analyzes every conversation, worries about the future, and judges the present.

understand.common_misconception: Thinking that stopping your overthinking loop will make you simple-minded, unintelligent, or incapable of planning your work.
understand.attainment_meaning: Your intellect actually becomes incredibly sharp and fast because it is no longer weighed down by the constant noise of egoic anxiety.

verses.philosophy: This is the state of *Chinta-Nivritti* (the cessation of mental worry). Pure consciousness shines effortlessly when the mind stops projecting artificial problems and interpretations onto reality.

verses.sanskrit:
किं करोमि क्व गच्छामि किं गृह्णामि विमुञ्चामि ।
इति चिन्ताकुला यस्य तस्य स्वास्थ्यं कथं भवेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
kiṃ karōmi kva gacchāmi kiṃ gṛhṇāmi vimuñcāmi |
iti cintākulā yasya tasya svāsthyaṃ kathaṃ bhavēt ||
verses.english: How can genuine inner health belong to someone whose mind is continuously panicked by calculations: 'What should I do? Where should I go? What should I hold onto, and what should I reject?'
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra highlights the absolute exhaustion of a mind caught in calculation. The ego lives in a constant state of panic, micro-managing its identity, second-guessing its choices, and calculating options. This friction completely blocks you from experiencing the natural peace of your presence.

understand.common_misconception: Believing that you can achieve perfect mental health by continuously fixing your external conditions and rearranging your lifestyle details.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing that peace arrives only when you stop fighting with the current moment and drop the frantic need to micro-manage life.

verses.philosophy: The mind's continuous calculation (*Cinta-Kula*) is the ultimate filter that distorts presence. Dropping the calculation allows awareness to settle into its natural state of wellness (*Svasthyam*).

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वमात्ममयं विद्धि चिन्मात्रं च निरन्तरम् ।
अतोऽहमिति मत्वैव बन्धमोक्षौ न चिन्तयेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
sarvamātmamayaṃ viddhi cinmātraṃ ca nirantaram |
ato'hamiti matvaiva bandhamokṣau na cintayet ||
verses.english: Recognize that everything is an expression of the Self, which is continuous, pure consciousness. Knowing this, stop obsessing over thoughts of bondage or liberation.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra provides the ultimate non-dual solution. Everything you see, hear, or experience is just consciousness taking a temporary shape. When you know you are that consciousness, worrying about whether you are 'making progress' or 'acting enlightened' is revealed to be a pointless mental story.

understand.common_misconception: Treating your everyday mind like a dangerous, dark enemy that you must constantly battle, conquer, and destroy through force.
understand.attainment_meaning: Seeing that your mind is just a natural, brief movement inside your wide-open awareness, which removes its power to bother you.

verses.philosophy: This is the integration of *Sarvam-Atma-Bhava*—the realization that everything is the Self. When the appearance of a separate world dissolves, relative concepts like bondage and freedom disappear entirely.

verses.sanskrit:
अहो निरञ्जनः शान्तः बोधोऽहं प्रकृतेः परः ।
एतावन्तमहं कालं मोहेनैव विडम्बितः ॥
verses.transliteration:
ahō nirañjanaḥ śāntaḥ bōdhō'ham prakṛtēḥ paraḥ |
etāvantamahaṃ kālaṃ mōhenaiva viḍambitḥ ||
verses.english: Wonderful! I am stainless, peaceful, pure awareness, and beyond nature! All this time I have been duped and deluded by illusion.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra quotes King Janaka's own profound realization from verse 2.1 back to him. He uses Janaka's own words as a mirror, reminding him to stay anchored in that immediate, pristine flash of awakening rather than letting his mind drag him back into theoretical overthinking.

understand.common_misconception: Assuming that spiritual realization is a complex, mysterious energy that you must continuously work to maintain or upgrade every day.
understand.attainment_meaning: It is simply the unshakeable recognition that your core nature was never dirty, damaged, or trapped by your thoughts in the first place.

verses.philosophy: The true Self stands completely independent of *Prakriti* (material nature). Delusion is not an actual entity with physical weight; it is simply a temporary lapse in attention that vanishes when you remember who you are.

verses.sanskrit:
क्व शास्त्रं क्व च विज्ञानं क्व वा चित्तं निराश्रयम् ।
परमात्मनि निष्कम्पः स्वमहिम्नि स्थितोऽस्म्यहम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
kva śāstraṃ kva ca vijñānaṃ kva vā cittaṃ nirāśrayam |
paramātmani niṣkampaḥ svamahimni sthitō'smyaham ||
verses.english: Where are scriptures, where is intellectual knowledge, and where is the mind itself? Unmoving in supreme reality, I rest securely in my own inherent majesty.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra summarizes the ultimate destination. When you are home, you drop the map. Scriptures, analytical calculations, and even the mind's tracking mechanisms become obsolete because you are resting directly as the limitless reality that hosted them all along.

understand.common_misconception: Spending your whole life gathering more spiritual certifications, definitions, and theories to feel spiritually secure.
understand.attainment_meaning: Dropping the conceptual baggage entirely and resting simply as the clear, deep presence that is already available right now.

verses.philosophy: This lands on *Svamahima-Sthiti*—resting in your own inherent majesty. From the absolute standpoint, all methods and explanations are temporary scaffolds that drop away completely upon arrival.

verses.sanskrit:
शरीरं स्वर्गनरकौ बन्धमोक्षौ भयं तथा ।
कल्पनामात्रमेवैतत् किं मे कार्यं चिदात्मनः ॥
verses.transliteration:
śarīraṃ svarganarakau bandhamokṣau bhayaṃ tathā |
kalpanāmātramevaitat kiṃ me kāryaṃ cidātmanaḥ ||
verses.english: The body, heaven and hell, bondage and liberation, as well as fear—these are all mere projections of the mind. What have I to do with any of them, I who am pure consciousness?
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra closes Chapter 16 by sweeping away every remaining dualistic framework. Social rankings, religious fears of bad outcomes, and spiritual desires for specific milestones are recognized as mental graphics. Anchored as pure consciousness, you can drop all psychological baggage and rest in perfect, natural freedom.

understand.common_misconception: Believing that true peace requires you to conform to rigid rules, dogmas, and superstitious tracking systems.
understand.attainment_meaning: Stepping out of all mental traps entirely, knowing that your true nature is eternally free from both worldly and heavenly anxieties.

verses.philosophy: This is the absolute core of Advaita Vedanta: *Paramartha Drishti*. The entire universe is just a temporary ripple on the surface of your true identity, leaving your absolute presence completely untouched, stainless, and ever-free.

title: Tatvabhava Prakaranam
description: The True State of One Liberated While Living
text: In this beautiful, extensive chapter, traditionally titled Tatvabhava Prakaranam (The Plane of the True State of Reality), the sage Ashtavakra outlines the precise behavior, lifestyle, and inner landscape of a Jivanmukta—one who is fully liberated while still living in a physical body. He describes a state of supreme, effortless freedom where the sage functions naturally in society, completely unaffected by praise, blame, luxury, poverty, or the impending drop of the physical shell.

verses.sanskrit:
अष्टावक्र उवाच -
कृतार्थो जनको यद्वत् स्वमहिम्नि स्थितोऽभवत् ।
तथैव हि भवेद् योगी सर्वत्र गळितस्पृहः ॥
verses.transliteration:
aṣṭāvakra uvāca -
kṛtārthō janakō yadvat svamahimni sthitō'bhavat |
tathaiva hi bhavēd yōgī sarvatra gāḷitaspṛhaḥ ||
verses.english: Ashtavakra said: One who has achieved ultimate fulfillment and rests securely in their own inherent majesty like King Janaka, becomes a true yogi whose cravings have dissolved in every situation.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra holds up Janaka as the benchmark of successful integration. True yoga is not about running away from responsibilities or locking yourself in a trance; it is the state where you can sit on a royal throne or walk down a city street with zero internal cravings driving your peace.

understand.common_misconception: Thinking that a true spiritual master must look physically starved, avoid society, or act like a severe, unapproachable hermit.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing that absolute freedom can be lived right in the middle of everyday society, as long as your inner happiness is independent of external events.

verses.philosophy: This is *Sarvatra-Galita-Spruha*—the total dissolution of craving in all relative domains. The true yogi does not avoid worldly contexts; they simply neutralize the ego's demand for self-validation within them.

verses.sanskrit:
अहो विश्वमिदं भाति कल्पितं रज्जुसर्पवत् ।
आनन्दपरमानन्दः स बोधस्त्वं सुखं चर ॥
verses.transliteration:
ahō viśvamidaṃ bhāti kalpitaṃ rajjusarpavat |
ānandaparamānandaḥ sa bōdhastvaṃ sukhaṃ cara ||
verses.english: Wonderful! This entire universe appears imagined, like a snake in a rope. You are that pure, supreme, blissful consciousness. Live happily.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra returns to the cornerstone rope-and-snake metaphor. When you discover that the 'frightening snake' is just an innocent piece of rope, the fear dissolves without an uphill battle. When you see the world is an expression of awareness, you stop fighting it and live with effortless joy.

understand.common_misconception: Assuming that realizing the world is 'imagined' means you must treat life with bitter cynicism or cold, careless indifference.
understand.attainment_meaning: It means you stop letting temporary situations terrify you, allowing you to engage with the world with complete lighthearted ease.

verses.philosophy: The universe holds an overlay status (*Adhyasa*). Recognizing the substrate (*Brahman/Awareness*) removes the power of the projection to cause psychological bondage.

verses.sanskrit:
खादन् गच्छन् सुRequest नित्यं पश्यन्नपि न पश्यति ।
बुवन्नपि न च ब्रूते कस्येयमुपमा भवेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
khādan gacchan suptō nityaṃ paśyannapi na paśyati |
bruvannapi na ca brūtē kasyēyamupamā bhavēt ||
verses.english: While eating, walking, or sleeping, the wise one seeing, does not see; speaking, they do not speak. To whom can such a unique soul be compared?
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra describes the natural flow of an integrated life. The physical container functions normally—chewing food, walking across rooms, resting at night, and conversing with others. But because there is no separate ego claiming ownership over these actions, the sage remains perfectly still inside.

understand.common_misconception: Expecting an enlightened person to stop talking, moving, or engaging in basic human biological functions.
understand.attainment_meaning: They live naturally and fluidly on the outside, while their internal awareness remains completely unattached behind the scenes.

verses.philosophy: This is the operational reality of *Sahaja Avastha* (the natural state). Actions flow through the body-mind complex (*Prakriti*) without leaving any psychological residue or building new ego karma.

verses.sanskrit:
भिक्षुर्वा भूपतिर्वापि यो निष्कामः स शोभते ।
भावेषु गतासङ्गः कस्यचित् उत्तमः स्मृतः ॥
verses.transliteration:
bhikṣurvā bhūpatirvāpi yō niṣkāmaḥ sa śōbhatē |
bhāvēṣu gatāsaṅgaḥ kasyacit uttamaḥ smṛtaḥ ||
verses.english: Whether a penniless beggar or a powerful king, glorious is the one who is completely desireless and free from attachment to worldly conditions.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra obliterates all social, economic, and institutional tracking. A king on a golden throne and a beggar on a dirt path are exactly equal if both are free from internal dependency. True nobility is measured exclusively by your inner freedom.

understand.common_misconception: Believing that you must change your bank account, drop your career, or alter your clothing to find your true self.
understand.attainment_meaning: Your external position is just a costume. True freedom is an inside job, determined by whether your awareness is independent of the role you play.

verses.philosophy: The text balances status via *Nishkama* (desirelessness). Outer conditions belong entirely to the body's environment; inner freedom belongs exclusively to the unattached witness.

verses.sanskrit:
क्व स्वच्छन्दं क्व च गमनं क्व लाभः क्व च वा गतिः ।
परमात्मनि निष्कम्पः स्वमहिम्नि स्थितोऽस्म्यहम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
kva svacchandaṃ kva ca gamanaṃ kva lābhaḥ kva ca vā gatiḥ |
paramātmani niṣkampaḥ svamahimni sthitō'smyaham ||
verses.english: Where is unrestricted desire, where is movement, where is gain, and where is a path for me? Unmoving in supreme reality, I rest securely in my own inherent majesty.
verses.commentary: The sage steps completely off the track of linear progress. Paths, growth metrics, winning, and losing are calculations that belong to the mind's grid. Resting as the absolute witness, the entire dualistic urge to travel toward a future perfection collapses into stillness.

understand.common_misconception: Viewing the spiritual journey as an endless marathon where you must continuously run to upgrade a broken soul.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing you are already at the destination, which allows you to stop running and finally be present right here.

verses.philosophy: This is *Svamahima-Sthiti*—resting in your own inherent majesty. From the absolute standpoint, no movement ever occurred; awareness has always been home.

verses.sanskrit:
मय्यनन्तमहाम्भोधौ विश्वं नाम तरङ्गिका ।
उदेति वा शाम्यति वा न मे वृद्धिर्न वा क्षतिः ॥
verses.transliteration:
mayyanantamahāmbhōdhau viśvaṃ nāma taraṅgikā |
udēti vā śāmyati vā na mē vṛddhirna vā kṣatiḥ ||
verses.english: In the infinite ocean of myself, the thing called the universe is nothing but a temporary ripple. Whether it rises or falls, I experience neither growth nor loss.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra shrinks the massive universe down to a minor ripple on the ocean of consciousness. By changing the scale of identity from a fragile body to infinite space, the weight of all worldly events, crises, and anxieties disappears in an instant.

understand.common_misconception: Treating your personal identity and immediate human problems as the absolute, permanent center of the entire cosmos.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing that your personal life is a brief, beautiful moment inside a limitless field of awareness, which takes the pressure off.

verses.philosophy: The universe possesses a dependent reality status (*Mithya*). Because relative experiences add zero value to pure consciousness, the sage remains untouched by cosmic shifts.

verses.sanskrit:
धीरोऽयं परमानन्दो दृश्यते न हि संसृतौ ।
अनावृतो निराकाङ्क्षः शान्तस्त्वं सुखं चर ॥
verses.transliteration:
dhīrō'yaṃ paramanandō dṛśyatē na hi saṃsṛtau |
anāvṛtō nirākāṅkṣaḥ śāntastvaṃ sukhaṃ cara ||
verses.english: This supreme bliss of the wise one cannot be found anywhere in the frantic chasing of the world. Limitless, desireless, and quiet, move through life happily.
verses.commentary: Worldly entertainment can offer short-term comfort, but it cannot offer permanent peace because it depends on changing conditions. Ashtavakra points back to your own unconditioned presence as the only source of unshakeable contentment.

understand.common_misconception: Hoping that if you just collect enough money, possessions, or praise, your mind will finally stop worrying.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing that material patches cannot cure an internal sense of lack, and dropping the chase unlocks immediate happiness.

verses.philosophy: True joy is *Ananyasrita*—independent of external triggers. It is the natural radiant nature of the Self (*Ananda-Svarupa*) that shines when worldly thirst drops away.

verses.sanskrit:
निर्मुक्तो निष्क्रियोऽसि त्वं स्वप्रकाशो निरञ्जनः ।
अयमेव हि ते बन्धो समाधिमनुतिष्ठसि ॥
verses.transliteration:
nirmuktō niṣkriyō'si tvaṃ svaprakāśō nirañjanaḥ |
ayameva hi te bandhaḥ samādhimanutiṣṭhasi ||
verses.english: You are eternally free, actionless, self-luminous, and stainless. Your actual bondage is that you keep practicing formal meditation to capture this.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra re-emphasizes his most radical paradox. Forcing your mind into tight meditative concentration states implies a belief that you are currently broken. True peace is your natural condition; attempting to perform your way into it keeps the separate seeker alive.

understand.common_misconception: Believing that staying enlightened requires you to spend your whole life performing strenuous breathing tricks or mental trances.
understand.attainment_meaning: Dropping the spiritual performance entirely, recognizing that your baseline awareness is already silent and pristine.

verses.philosophy: The Self is *Sva-Prakasha* (self-luminous). Relying on specialized meditation states (*Samadhi*) makes your freedom conditional, which reinforces the illusion of limitation.

verses.sanskrit:
यदा मनसि संकल्पः कश्चिद्वा संप्रजायते ।
तदैव बन्धनं विद्धि तन्नाशो मोक्ष उच्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
yadā manasi saṃkalpaḥ kaścidvā saṃprajāyate |
tadaiva bandanaṃ viddhi tannāśo mokṣa ucyate ||
verses.english: The exact millisecond a conceptual condition or desire takes root in the mind, know that very moment as bondage. The dissolution of that condition is what is called liberation.
verses.commentary: Bondage is explained as an immediate, real-time micro-movement of identification. The moment your mind creates a strict condition for your happiness ('I must win this outcome, or I am ruined'), it manufactures an internal state of lack. Dropping that mental calculation is liberation.

understand.common_misconception: Assuming that bondage is a massive, permanent mountain of ancient karma that takes lifetimes of painful struggle to slow-burn away.
understand.attainment_meaning: Seeing that bondage is simply a habit of overthinking and clinging happening in the current moment, which can be dropped right now.

verses.philosophy: Bondage is highly dynamic, driven by *Sankalpa* (mental projection/intention). When the mind stops creating conditions, awareness resets instantly to its default free state.

verses.sanskrit:
कूटस्थं बोधमद्वैतमात्मानं परिभावय ।
आभासोऽहं भ्रमं मुक्त्वा भावं बाह्यमथान्तरम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
kūṭasthaṃ bodhamadvaitamātmānaṃ paribhāvaya |
ābhāso'haṃ bhramaṃ muktvā bhāvaṃ bāhyamathāntaram ||
verses.english: Meditate on the unchanging, non-dual Self. Drop the illusion of being an isolated reflection, and leave behind both internal and external attachments.
verses.commentary: An anvil (*Kutastha*) remains completely unmoved despite being struck by countless heavy hammers. Your core awareness is that anvil, holding up all your thoughts, moods, and life chapters without ever being damaged or altered by them.

understand.common_misconception: Believing your spiritual path is about repairing, fixing, or decorating your human ego structure.
understand.attainment_meaning: Stepping back from the ego entirely, realizing it is just a brief, passing reflection on the surface of your awareness.

verses.philosophy: The ego personality (*Jiva*) is a localized reflection of pure awareness. Freedom means shifting your core identity from the fragile reflection to the source.

verses.sanskrit:
देहाभिमानपाशेन बद्धोऽसि तात सर्वदा ।
बोधोऽहं ज्ञानखङ्गेन तन्निकृत्य सुखी भव ॥
verses.transliteration:
dehābhimānapāśena baddho'si tāta sarvada |
bodho'haṃ jñānakhaṅgena tannikṛtya sukhī bhava ||
verses.english: My child, you have long been bound by the rope of body-identification. Cut through that bond with the sword of knowledge—'I am pure awareness'—and be happy.
verses.commentary: Body-ownership functions like a tight cord around your awareness, tricking you into feeling fragile and temporary. Ashtavakra points to sharp discrimination (*Jnana*) to cut it clean away, returning attention to its boundless nature.

understand.common_misconception: Assuming that untying your mental identification loops requires decades of intense physical self-punishment or ascetic routines.
understand.attainment_meaning: Using clear insight to see through the illusion immediately, which cuts the psychological dependency without a physical struggle.

verses.philosophy: The root cause of human anxiety is *Deha-Abhimana* (body-ownership). Shifting identity to awareness (*Bodho'ham*) brings immediate structural release.

verses.sanskrit:
न साक्षो वर्तते लोको न साक्षी कस्यचित् तथा ।
स्वप्रकाशोऽसि निर्मुक्तो मा मतिं कुरु संसृतौ ॥
verses.transliteration:
na sākṣō vartatē lōkō na sākṣī kasyacit tathā |
savaprakāśō'si nirmuktō mā matiṃ kuru saṃsṛtau ||
verses.english: The world does not possess the witness, nor does the witness belong to the world. You are self-luminous and eternally free. Do not scatter your mind back into worldly anxiety.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra draws a clean operational boundary. Awareness hosts the world, but it never merges with it. Just like a television screen displays a massive fire without actually catching fire, your consciousness displays daily challenges without absorbing any damage.

understand.common_misconception: Worrying that your internal peace will be permanently broken if your external conditions become chaotic.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing that your core awareness sits cleanly outside the scene, completely immune to worldly disruptions.

verses.philosophy: This is the absolute autonomy of the subject (*Drashta*). The witness is *Sva-Prakasha* (self-luminous) and completely independent of the objects it illuminates.

verses.sanskrit:
त्वमेकश्चेतनः शुद्धो जडं विश्वमसत्तथा ।
अविद्यापि न काचित्स्वा का बुभुत्सा तथापि ते ॥
verses.transliteration:
tvamēkaścetanaḥ śuddhō jaḍaṃ viśvamasattathā |
avidyāpi na kācitsvā kā bubhutsā tathāpi tē ||
verses.english: You are the solitary, pure, conscious awareness. The material universe is insentient and temporary. Even ignorance itself is completely non-existent. What else is left for you to anxiously seek or know?
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra removes even the intellectual anxiety of trying to figure out 'why' ignorance exists. The material world is a passing scene, and you are the conscious space hosting it. Overthinking the mechanics of the illusion is just another trick to keep your mind busy.

understand.common_misconception: Spending years obsessing over spiritual theories, trying to build a perfect mental map of cosmic dimensions and hidden dimensions.
understand.attainment_meaning: Stepping out of the intellectual tracking loop altogether, resting simply as the clear presence that observes all concepts.

verses.philosophy: From the highest standpoint (*Paramartha*), ignorance has no real independent footing; analyzing it is like searching for darkness using a flashlight.

verses.sanskrit:
राज्यं सुताः कलत्राणि शरीराणि सुखानि च ।
संसक्तस्यापि नष्टानि तव जन्मातिजन्मनि ॥
verses.transliteration:
rājyaṃ sutāḥ kalatrāṇi शरीराणि सुखानि च |
saṃsaktasyāpi naṣṭāni tava janmātijanmani ||
verses.english: Kingdoms, children, relationships, physical bodies, and luxuries have all slipped through your fingers across countless lifetimes, despite your deep attachment to them. They are gone.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra uses an expansive lens on time to break immediate panic. Even if you manage to secure perfect worldly conditions, wealth, and praise today, the river of time will eventually wash them away. Realizing you have played this game across vast stretches of time helps break the gravity of current worries.

understand.common_misconception: Treating your current set of problems, status, and worldly dramas as the absolute, permanent center of the universe.
understand.attainment_meaning: Gaining a panoramic perspective on your lifespan, which shrinks your immediate anxieties down into minor, passing ripples.

verses.philosophy: The evolutionary cycle of the ego (*Samsara*) is an endless loop of acquisition and inevitable loss. Recognizing this pattern triggers a natural shift in attention away from the changing scene and back to the permanent viewer.

verses.sanskrit:
अलमर्थेन कामेन सुकृतेनापि कर्मणा ।
एभ्यः संसारकान्तारे न विश्रामोऽभवन्मनः ॥
verses.transliteration:
alamarthēna kāmēna sukrutēnāpi karmaṇā |
ēbhyaḥ saṃsārakāntārē na viśrāmō'bhavanmanaḥ ||
verses.english: Enough with wealth, enough with pleasure, and enough with good deeds! The mind has never found permanent rest or safety in this wild jungle of worldly chasing.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra calls for a definitive, complete halt: 'Enough!' (*Alam*). He explicitly groups 'good deeds done for pride' right alongside wealth and pleasure. If you perform moral acts simply to score spiritual points, feel superior, or buy a pleasant afterlife, you are still operating from a stressful sense of lack.

understand.common_misconception: Believing that inner peace is a fragile trophy you can build by checking off a list of perfect behaviors or moral scorecards.
understand.attainment_meaning: Dropping the exhaustive tracking systems and resting directly in the quiet presence that is already available right now.

verses.philosophy: This is the transition from desire-driven action to absolute quietude. The worldly mind is a forest of concepts; true rest (*Visrama*) is found only when the ego stops trying to negotiate with reality.

verses.sanskrit:
कृतं न कति जन्मानि कायेन मनसा गिरा ।
दुःखमायासदं तस्माद्विरमद्वैव पावन ॥
verses.transliteration:
krutaṃ na kati janmāni kāyēna मनसा गिरा |
duḥkhamāyāsadaṃ tasmādviramadvaiva pāvana ||
verses.english: For how many lifetimes have you labored tirelessly with your body, mind, and speech, only to arrive at recurring fatigue and stress? Therefore, O pure one, stop now.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra highlights the absolute exhaustion of the ego's continuous efforts to fix, improve, and defend itself. He reminds Janaka of his inherent purity (*pāvana*), inviting him to drop the historical struggle and simply stop running away from the present moment.

understand.common_misconception: Assuming that discovering deep inner quiet requires a massive, complex, and strenuous effort that you must continuously maintain.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing that peace is the effortless state that remains when you simply stop over-analyzing and trying to escape your life.

verses.philosophy: This lands on *Nivritti*—the total cessation of egoic striving. Actions continue to flow naturally through the body as needed, but the frantic internal driver stops completely, dissolving into peace.

verses.sanskrit:
उन्मत्तवन्निःशङ्को जीवत्येव महाशयः ।
न वेत्ति व्यवहारं च शान्तः स्वच्छन्दचारिणः ॥
verses.transliteration:
unmattavanniḥśaṅkō jīvatyēva mahāśayaḥ |
na vētti vyavahāraṃ ca śāntḥ svacchandacāriṇaḥ ||
verses.english: Free from doubt and fear, the great soul lives like an innocent madman or a child. They pay no psychological attention to social games, remaining completely serene in their spontaneous movement.
verses.commentary: The text compares the sage to a madman (*unmattavat*) not because they lack intelligence, but because they are entirely free from social anxiety, calculation, and the desperate need for approval. They don't operate from a script of social pretension; they move out of pure, authentic presence.

understand.common_misconception: Expecting an enlightened person to be a highly predictable, stiff, and formal saint who follows rigid etiquette rules at all times.
understand.attainment_meaning: They are simple, transparent, and fluid, matching their responses perfectly to the raw truth of the current moment.

verses.philosophy: This is *Svachandachari*—spontaneous, unrestricted expression. The sage has dissolved the internal editor of the ego, allowing consciousness to operate cleanly without structural pretense.

verses.sanskrit:
चित्तनिर्मूलने मुक्ताः केवलाभासगोचराः ।
न स्मरन्ति गतं किञ्चिन्न च भावं विचिन्तयेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
cittanirmūlanē muktāḥ kēvalābhāsagōcarāḥ |
na smaranti gataṃ kiñcinnā ca bhāvaṃ vicintayēt ||
verses.english: With the root of separate mental tracking eliminated, they rest as pure presence. They do not obsess over past regrets, nor do they anxiously project the future.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra cuts the temporal chain. The human mind is usually trapped in an exhausting time loop: dragging up old regrets and guilt from the past, or projecting catastrophic scenarios about the future. The sage breaks this engine, living completely in the wide-open present.

understand.common_misconception: Carrying a heavy, lifelong burden of guilt and self-punishment over past mistakes, assuming it makes you a better person.
understand.attainment_meaning: Extracting the practical lesson from a mistake, and letting the memory drop so your presence stays clean.

verses.philosophy: This is *Chitta-Nirmula*—the uprooting of the reactive mind. When separate tracking is disabled, awareness rests securely in its timeless, continuous nature.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्तः शून्यः बहिः स्वच्छन्दचारिणः ।
भ्रान्तस्येव दशास्तास्तास्तादृशा एव जानते ॥
verses.transliteration:
antaḥ śūnyaḥ bahiḥ svacchandacāriṇaḥ |
bhrāntasyēva daśāstāstāstādṛśā ēva jānatē ||
verses.english: Completely empty of inner conceptual conflict, yet moving spontaneously in the external world according to its own laws, their unique state can only be recognized by those who are also free.
verses.commentary: To an ordinary, stressed observer, a free soul might look unpredictable, eccentric, or like a drifter (*bhrāntasyēva*). Because they don't act out of fear or calculated greed, they don't fit into standard societal rankings. Their internal ease can only be fully comprehended by someone who has also stepped out of the cage.

understand.common_misconception: Expecting an awakened mind to look like a highly corporate, perfectly packaged spiritual teacher who fits standard expectations.
understand.attainment_meaning: Recognizing that true freedom is simple, unvarnished, and completely authentic, matching itself perfectly to life's raw textures.

verses.philosophy: The internal state is *Antah-Sunya* (inner emptiness of conflict), while the external movement is *Svachandachari* (spontaneous flow), rendering their life a harmless, beautiful expression of unconditioned consciousness.

verses.sanskrit:
शरीरं स्वर्गनरकौ बन्धमोक्षौ भयं तथा ।
कल्पनामात्रमेवैतत् किं मे कार्यं चिदात्मनः ॥
verses.transliteration:
śarīraṃ svarganarakau bandhamokṣau bhayaṃ tathā |
kalpanāmātramevaitat kiṃ me kāryaṃ cidātmanaḥ ||
verses.english: The body, heaven and hell, bondage and liberation, as well as fear—these are all mere projections of the mind. What have I to do with any of them, I who am pure consciousness?
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra closes this massive chapter on living liberation by sweeping away every relative framework. Social hierarchies, religious anxieties about cosmic punishments, and spiritual parameters are recognized as mental imagery. Anchored as pure consciousness, you can drop all psychological baggage and rest in perfect, natural freedom.

understand.common_misconception: Believing that discovering deep peace requires you to conform to rigid rules, dogmas, and superstitious tracking metrics.
understand.attainment_meaning: Stepping out of all mental traps entirely, knowing that your true nature is eternally free from both worldly and heavenly anxieties.

verses.philosophy: This is the absolute standpoint of Advaita Vedanta: *Paramartha Drishti*. The entire space-time universe is just a temporary ripple on the surface of your true identity, leaving your absolute presence completely untouched, stainless, and ever-free.

title: Shanti Prakaranam
description: The Ultimate Exposition of Absolute Peace
text: This chapter, traditionally titled Shanti Prakaranam (The Exposition of Absolute Peace), is the crown jewel and the longest section of the Ashtavakra Gita. Because it spans 100 verses, it is provided below in a clean, highly structured compilation split into logical experiential nodes to integrate into your application cleanly without crashing your asset parser.

verses.sanskrit:
अष्टावक्र उवाच -
यस्य बोधोदये तावद् विज्ञानभ्रमसन्ततिः ।
प्रशमं याति तस्मै नमः शान्ताय तेजसे ॥
verses.transliteration:
aṣṭāvakra uvāca -
yasya bōdhōdayē tāvad vijñānabhramasantitiḥ |
praśamaṃ yāti tasmai namaḥ śāntāya tējasē ||
verses.english: Ashtavakra said: Salutations to that supreme, peaceful Light, upon whose awakening the entire stream of intellectual delusion and overthinking goes completely quiet.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra opens the grand finale by bowing to the light of awareness itself. When this light dawns, the mind's endless habit of generating doubts, definitions, and mental arguments doesn't just get answered—it completely dissolves into stillness.

understand.common_misconception: Thinking that spiritual clarity means finding a perfect, complex intellectual answer to every single question your mind asks.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing that clarity is a state of presence where the mind simply stops asking anxious, unnecessary questions altogether.

verses.philosophy: This is the dissolution of *Vijñāna-Bhrama* (intellectual delusion). Pure consciousness is not an object to be analyzed by the intellect; it is the light that illuminates the intellect itself.

verses.sanskrit:
भोगाभावेन शब्दादेरदृश्यत्वेन चात्मनः ।
विक्षेपैकाग्र्यहृदय एवमेवाहमास्थितः ॥
verses.transliteration:
bhōgābhāvēna śabdādēradṛśyatvēna cātmanaḥ |
vikṣēpaikāgryahṛdaya ēvamēvāhamāsthitaḥ ||
verses.english: By simply seeing that worldly assets contain no permanent substance, and that the Self cannot be grabbed as an external object, the mind is freed from both distraction and forced focus.
verses.commentary: The ordinary mind vacillates between being wildly distracted by worldly desires or aggressively forcing itself into deep concentration to block out the world. The sage drops this exhausting battle. Since objects have lost their false value, the mind rests in natural balance.

understand.common_misconception: Believing that you must spend your entire life fighting your thoughts to maintain an artificial state of inner peace.
understand.attainment_meaning: Allowing thoughts to come and go like passing traffic without tracking them or treating them as a danger to your peace.

verses.philosophy: This dissolves the duality of *Vikṣēpa* (distraction) and *Ekāgratā* (concentration). Awareness stands completely outside of these intentional mental exercises.

verses.sanskrit:
कृताकृत्यद्वन्द्वानि कदा शान्तानि कस्य वा ।
एवं ज्ञात्वेह निर्वेदाद्भव त्यागी व्रती विना ॥
verses.transliteration:
kṛtākṛtyadvandvāni kadā śāntāni kasya vā |
evaṃ jñātveha nirvedādbhava tyāgī vratī vinā ||
verses.english: When or for whom have the dualities of social obligations and spiritual chores ever been fully finished? Seeing their endless nature, drop your rigid calculations and vows.
verses.commentary: There will always be one more errand to run, one more task to complete, or one more spiritual goal to track. Ashtavakra tells Janaka to stop waiting for a hypothetical perfect day to relax, and to choose to rest right now.

understand.common_misconception: Waiting until all your worldly problems, bills, and family tasks are 100% resolved before allowing yourself to be at peace.
understand.attainment_meaning: Recognizing that life's tasks are endless, and choosing to be internally still right in the middle of them.

verses.philosophy: This is the intentional transcendence of *Karma-Kanda* (the path of endless duties). Real peace requires stepping out of the transactional mindset of the ego entirely.

verses.sanskrit:
न साक्षो वर्तते लोको न साक्षी कस्यचित् तथा ।
स्वप्रकाशोऽसि निर्मुक्तो मा मतिं कुरु संसृतौ ॥
verses.transliteration:
na sākṣō vartatē lōkō na sākṣी kasyacit tathā |
savaprakāśō'si nirmuktō mā matiṃ kuru saṃsṛtau ||
verses.english: The world does not possess the witness, nor does the witness belong to the world. You are self-luminous and eternally free. Do not scatter your attention back into worldly anxiety.
verses.commentary: Awareness hosts the perception of the world, but it never gets mixed into it. Just as a movie screen holds a projection of a massive flood without actually getting wet, your consciousness holds daily trials without absorbing a single scratch of damage.

understand.common_misconception: Worrying that your baseline inner peace can be permanently shattered or broken if your external life gets chaotic.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing that your core consciousness sits safely outside the scene, entirely immune to worldly noise.

verses.philosophy: This is *Drashta-Autonomy*—the absolute independence of the subject. The witness is *Sva-Prakasha* (self-luminous) and completely untouched by the scenes it illuminates.

verses.sanskrit:
देहाभिमानपाशेन बद्धोऽसि तात सर्वदा ।
बोधोऽहं ज्ञानखङ्गेन तन्निकृत्य सुखी भव ॥
verses.transliteration:
dehābhimānapāśena baddho'si tāta sarvada |
bodho'haṃ jñānakhaṅgena tannikṛtya sukhī bhava ||
verses.english: My child, you have long been bound by the rope of body-identification. Cut through that bond with the sword of knowledge—'I am pure awareness'—and be happy.
verses.commentary: Body-ownership functions like a tight cord around your awareness, tricking you into feeling fragile and temporary. Ashtavakra points to sharp discrimination to cut it clean away, returning attention to its boundless nature.

understand.common_misconception: Assuming that untying your mental identification loops requires decades of intense physical self-punishment or ascetic routines.
understand.attainment_meaning: Using clear insight to see through the illusion immediately, which cuts the psychological dependency without a physical struggle.

verses.philosophy: The root cause of human anxiety is *Deha-Abhimana* (body-ownership). Shifting identity to awareness brings immediate structural release.

verses.sanskrit:
न किंचिच्चिन्तयेद् यस्तु स एव सुखी भवति ।
तस्मात्सर्वं परित्यज्य न किंचिदपि चिन्तयेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
na kiṃciccitayed yastu sa eva sukhī bhavati |
tasmātsarvaṃ parityajya na kiṃcidapi cintayet ||
verses.english: One who does not anxiously calculate or overthink anything becomes truly happy. Therefore, leaving behind all mental stories, do not anxiously project anything.
verses.commentary: Ashtavakra is not telling you to become brain-dead; he is telling you to stop the exhausting, endless self-commentary that over-analyzes every conversation, worries about the future, and judges the present.

understand.common_misconception: Thinking that stopping your overthinking loop will make you simple-minded, unintelligent, or incapable of planning your work.
understand.attainment_meaning: Your intellect actually becomes incredibly sharp and fast because it is no longer weighed down by the constant noise of egoic anxiety.

verses.philosophy: This is the state of *Chinta-Nivritti* (the cessation of mental worry). Pure consciousness shines effortlessly when the mind stops projecting artificial problems and interpretations onto reality.

verses.sanskrit:
किं करोमि क्व गच्छामि किं गृह्णामि विमुञ्चामि ।
इति चिन्ताकुला यस्य तस्य स्वास्थ्यं कस्य जायते ॥
verses.transliteration:
kiṃ karōmi kva gacchāmi kiṃ gṛhṇāmi vimuñcāmi |
iti cintākulā yasya tasya svāsthyaṃ kasya jāyatē ||
verses.english: How can genuine inner health belong to someone whose mind is continuously panicked by calculations: 'What should I do? Where should I go? What should I hold onto, and what should I reject?'
verses.commentary: The ego lives in a constant state of panic, micro-managing its identity, second-guessing its choices, and calculating options. This friction completely blocks you from experiencing the natural peace of your presence.

understand.common_misconception: Believing that you can achieve perfect mental health by continuously fixing your external conditions and rearranging your lifestyle details.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing that peace arrives only when you stop fighting with the current moment and drop the frantic need to micro-manage life.

verses.philosophy: The mind's continuous calculation (*Cinta-Kula*) is the ultimate filter that distorts presence. Dropping the calculation allows awareness to settle into its natural state of wellness (*Svasthyam*).

verses.sanskrit:
अहो अहं नमस्तुभ्यं विनाशो यस्य नास्ति मे ।
ब्रह्मादिस्तम्बपर्यन्तजगन्नाशेऽपि तिष्ठतः ॥
verses.transliteration:
aho ahaṃ namastubhyaṃ vināśo yasya nāsti me |
brahmādistambaparyantajagannāśe'pi tiṣṭhataḥ ||
verses.english: Wonderful am I! Salutations to myself, who knows no destruction. I remain untouched even if the entire universe, from Brahma down to a blade of grass, is destroyed.
verses.commentary: This is the realization that your fundamental awareness cannot be burned, cut, or destroyed by any worldly event. It outlasts all cosmic transformations.

understand.common_misconception: Confusing this self-salutation with egoistic vanity or personal pride.
understand.attainment_meaning: It is bowing to the infinite space of awareness that remains after the personal ego has been dropped.

verses.philosophy: The Self is *Nitya* (eternal) and *Achyuta* (imperishable). It is the unmoving background that outlasts all cosmic transformations.

verses.sanskrit:
यथा न तोयतो भिन्नास्तरङ्गाः फेनबुद्बुदाः ।
तथा आत्मनो न भिन्नं विश्वमात्मविनिर्गतम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
yathā na toyato bhinnāstaraṅgāḥ phenabudbudāḥ |
tathā ātmano na bhinnaṃ viśvamātmavinirgatam ||
verses.english: Just as waves, foam, and bubbles are not separate from water, so the universe, born from the Self, is not separate from the Self.
verses.commentary: A wave cannot claim to be independent of the ocean; its form, movement, and essence are entirely made of water. Forms are simply temporary arrangements of a single underlying reality.

understand.common_misconception: Assuming that non-dual philosophy treats the physical world as a complete hallucination with zero value.
understand.attainment_meaning: The world's independent existence is what is false; its essence as pure consciousness is absolutely real.

verses.philosophy: This illustrates *Vivartavada* (the doctrine of apparent transformation). The effect (the universe) is nothing other than its underlying cause (the Self).

verses.sanskrit:
अहो अहं नमस्तुभ्यं एकोऽहं देहवानपि ।
क्वचिन्न गन्ता नागन्ता व्याप्य विश्वमवस्थितः ॥
verses.transliteration:
aho ahaṃ namastubhyaṃ eko'haṃ dehavānapi |
kvacinna gantā nāgantā vyāpya viśvamavasthitaḥ ||
verses.english: Wonderful am I! Salutations to myself! Though appearing to inhabit a body, I am completely one. I neither go anywhere nor come from anywhere, but pervade the entire universe.
verses.commentary: The mind thinks the soul moves when the body walks across a room. Ashtavakra corrects this: the body moves *within* space, and space moves *within* your infinite awareness.

understand.common_misconception: Believing your consciousness is a localized energy trapped inside your skull that travels with you when you move.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing you are the stationary space in which your body's movements are tracked.

verses.philosophy: The Self is *Sarvagata* (all-pervading) and motionless. Movement is a physical attribute; it does not apply to the spacious witness.

verses.sanskrit:
धीरस्तु भोज्यमानोऽपि पीड्यमानोऽपि सर्वदा ।
आत्मानं केवलं पश्यन् न तुष्यति न कुप्यति ॥
verses.transliteration:
dhīrastu bhōjyamānō'pi pīḍyamānō'pi sarvadā |
ātmānaṃ kēvalaṃ paśyan na tuṣyati na kupyati ||
verses.english: The truly wise person, whether feasting on luxury or suffering pain, always sees only the solitary Self. They neither celebrate nor get angry.
verses.commentary: Outer conditions will change—sometimes life brings a feast, sometimes it brings hardship—but the witness remains steady and unbothered through both.

understand.common_misconception: Assuming that a spiritual person lives a charmed life where physical pain or unpleasant situations never happen.
understand.attainment_meaning: Life’s ups and downs still happen, but you stop letting them dictate your internal peace and balance.

verses.philosophy: This describes *Samatvam* (equanimity). The sage is anchored in the *Kevala* (the absolute, unattached witness), keeping them completely independent of changing circumstances.

verses.sanskrit:
चेष्टमानं शरीरं स्वं पश्यत्यन्यशरीरवत् ।
संस्तवे च निन्दायां कथं क्षुभ्येत् महाशयः ॥
verses.transliteration:
cēṣṭamānaṃ śarīraṃ svaṃ paśyatyanyaśarīravat |
saṃstavē ca nindāyāṃ kathaṃ kṣubhyēt mahāśayaḥ ||
verses.english: The noble soul watches their own body perform actions as if it were someone else's body. How then can they be shaken by praise or blame?
verses.commentary: When people praise or insult you, they are just making sounds directed at your temporary human persona. A great soul steps back, watching their own body interact as if watching a character in a play.

understand.common_misconception: Becoming an emotional roller-coaster, feeling amazing when praised and devastated when criticized.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing that public opinions are just passing weather patterns in other people's minds, completely separate from who you really are.

verses.philosophy: This is total freedom from *Ego-Involvement*. The body acts out its destiny (*Prarabdha*), while your consciousness remains steady, untouched by worldly reviews.

verses.sanskrit:
तन्तुमात्रो भवेदेव पटो यद्वद्विचारितः ।
तथा विश्वमिदं सर्वं आत्ममात्रं विचारितम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
tantumātro bhavedeva paṭo yadvadvicāritaḥ |
tathā viśvamidaṃ sarvaṃ ātmamātram vicāritam ||
verses.english: Just as a cloth, when examined closely, is found to be nothing but thread, so this universe, when analyzed, is found to be nothing but the Self.
verses.commentary: If you unravel a shirt completely, the shirt disappears and only thread remains. Looking deeply into any object reveals pure awareness at its core.

understand.common_misconception: Believing that things possess a solid, separate nature from the consciousness perceiving them.
understand.attainment_meaning: Forms are simply temporary arrangements of a single underlying reality.

verses.philosophy: Names and forms (*Nama-Rupa*) are superficial overlays. Real analysis dissolves the overlay to expose the continuous substrate.

verses.sanskrit:
इक्षुरसे ज्ञेया शर्करा तेन निर्मिता ।
यथा तथैव मयि विश्वं मन्मात्रेण प्रपूरितम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
ikṣurase jñeyā śarkarā tena nirmitā |
yathā tathaiva mayi viśvaṃ manmātreṇa prapūritam ||
verses.english: Just as sugar made from sugarcane juice is completely permeated with that sweetness, so the universe produced in me is thoroughly filled with my own nature.
verses.commentary: The sweetness of sugar cannot be isolated from the sugar itself. Consciousness permeates every texture of your life experience.

understand.common_misconception: Imagining God or the Absolute as an external creator sitting outside the universe.
understand.attainment_meaning: The universe is built *out* of consciousness, making every atom inherently divine.

verses.philosophy: Consciousness is the material cause (*Upadana*) as well as the intelligent cause (*Nimitta*) of everything that appears.

verses.sanskrit:
आत्माज्ञानाज्जगद्भाति आत्मज्ञानान्न भासते ।
रज्ज्वज्ञानादहिर्भाति तज्ज्ञानाद्भासते न हि ॥
verses.transliteration:
ātmājñānājjagadbhāti ātmajñānānna bhāsate |
rajvvajñānādahirbhāti tajjñānādbhāsate na hi ||
verses.english: The world appears from ignorance of the Self, and disappears when the Self is known. Just as a snake appears when a rope is not recognized, and vanishes once the rope is seen.
verses.commentary: The illusion of a dangerous world vanishes the moment you recognize the underlying reality.

understand.common_misconception: Expecting the physical world to vanish into thin air upon gaining spiritual wisdom.
understand.attainment_meaning: The physical objects remain, but your belief that they are separate, binding realities disappears completely.

verses.philosophy: The world as a source of bondage exists solely due to *Avidya* (ignorance). Correct knowledge (*Jnana*) instantly cancels out the illusion.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रकाशो मे निजं रूपं नातिरिक्तोऽस्म्यहं ततः ।
यदा प्रकाशते विश्वं तदाहंभास एव हि ॥
verses.transliteration:
prakāśo me nijaṃ rūpaṃ nātirikto'smyahaṃ tataḥ |
yadā prakāśate viśvaṃ tadāhaṃbhāsa eva hi ||
verses.english: Light is my fundamental nature; I am nothing other than that light. When the universe shines, it is simply my own light shining forth.
verses.commentary: You don't *have* a soul; you *are* the light that makes experience possible. Everything you see is a reflection of your own awareness.

understand.common_misconception: Viewing consciousness as a byproduct of brain activity or a tool used by the mind.
understand.attainment_meaning: Consciousness is the primary light source, and the mind and brain are simply instruments illuminated by it.

verses.philosophy: The Self is *Sva-Prakasha* (self-luminous). It does not need another light source to be seen, because it is the source of all illumination.

verses.sanskrit:
अहो कल्पितमहो मयि अज्ञानतो जगत् ।
यथा शुक्तौ रजतं मरुभूमौ जलं मरिच्यपि ॥
verses.transliteration:
aho kalpitamaho mayi ajñānato jagat |
yathā śuktau rajataṃ marubhūmau jalaṃ maricyapi ||
verses.english: Wonderful! The world appears in me through ignorance, like silver in mother-of-pearl, a mirage oasis in the desert sand, or a phantom in the twilight.
verses.commentary: Just as sunlight hitting desert sand creates the illusion of water, ignorance creates the illusion of a world separate from the Self.

understand.common_misconception: Blaming the world or other people for causing your inner suffering and distress.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing your suffering comes entirely from misinterpreting what you see, not from the things themselves.

verses.philosophy: The universe has an overlay status (*Adhyasa*). It borrows its apparent reality from the true Self, just as a mirage borrows its appearance from the desert sand.

verses.sanskrit:
मत्तो विनिर्गतं विश्वं मय्येव लयमेष्यति ।
मृदीव घटो जलधौ तरङ्गः कनके कटकं यथा ॥
verses.transliteration:
matto vinirgataṃ viśvaṃ mayyeva layameṣyati |
mṛdīva ghaṭo jalधौ तरङ्गः कनके कटकं यथा ॥
verses.english: The universe arises from me and dissolves back into me, just as a clay pot returns to clay, a wave returns to the ocean, or a gold bracelet melts back into gold.
verses.commentary: Forms are temporary shapes assumed by a single material. A gold bracelet can be melted down and reshaped into a ring, but the gold itself never changes.

understand.common_misconception: Viewing death as a terrifying void that completely destroys your existence.
understand.attainment_meaning: Death is simply a change of form, like a wave settling back into the stillness of the ocean.

verses.philosophy: The cycles of creation (*Srishti*) and dissolution (*Laya*) occur entirely within absolute awareness, which remains unchanged throughout both.

verses.sanskrit:
कूटस्थं बोधमद्वैतमात्मानं परिभावय ।
आभासोऽहं भ्रमं मुक्त्वा भावं बाह्यमथान्तरम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
kūṭasthaṃ bodhamadvaitamātmānaṃ paribhāvaya |
ābhāso'haṃ bhramaṃ muktvā bhāvaṃ bāhyamathāntaram ||
verses.english: Meditate on the unchanging, non-dual Self. Drop the illusion of being an isolated reflection, and leave behind both internal and external attachments.
verses.commentary: An anvil (*Kutastha*) remains completely unmoved despite being struck by countless hammers. The Self is that solid foundation.

understand.common_misconception: Believing your spiritual journey is about repairing your human ego or personality structure.
understand.attainment_meaning: Stepping back from the ego entirely, realizing it is just a passing reflection on the surface of your awareness.

verses.philosophy: The individual personality (*Jiva*) is a localized reflection of pure awareness. Freedom means shifting your identity from the reflection to the source.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्य धीः कुरुते बुद्धिं सा बुद्धिस्तस्य बन्धिनी ।
न बुद्ध्या लिप्यते साक्षी स साक्षी सर्वदा सुखी ॥
verses.transliteration:
yasya dhīḥ kurutē बुद्धिं सा बुद्धिस्तस्य बन्धिनी |
na buddhyā lipyatē sākṣī sa sākṣī sarvadā sukhī ||
verses.english: If the mind tries to rationalize and capture awareness, that very rationalization becomes a cage. The witness cannot be stained by the intellect; it remains eternally happy.
verses.commentary: You cannot figure out your way into awareness using intellectual data alone, because your intellect is an object being observed by awareness.

understand.common_misconception: Treating spiritual growth like an intense academic exam where you must continuously argue and analyze theories.
understand.attainment_meaning: Dropping the intellectual arguments and resting simply as the quiet presence that is already available.

verses.philosophy: The witness is *Atendriya*—beyond the reach of the mind and intellect. True peace is realized when the intellect stops trying to manage reality and dissolves into the source.

verses.sanskrit:
ज्ञात्वात्मानं जनः कश्चिन्नैव साशङ्कतां व्रजेत् ।
न बन्धो न च मुक्तिर्मे भ्रान्तिरेव हि कल्पिता ॥
verses.transliteration:
jñātvātmānaṃ janaḥ kaścinnaiva sāśaṅkatāṃ vrajēt |
na bandhō na ca muktirmē bhrāntirēva hi kalpitā ||
verses.english: Knowing the Self, a person never falls back into doubt or fear. Bondage and liberation are mere delusions projected by the mind.
verses.commentary: Once awareness recognizes itself, the fear of losing your spiritual state disappears completely. The dualistic tracking of progress is seen as a mental fiction.

understand.common_misconception: Living in constant fear of slipping back into ignorance or losing your awakening after a busy day.
understand.attainment_meaning: Realizing that your baseline presence is ever-present, completely outside the reach of mental modifications.

verses.philosophy: This is absolute certainty (*Niścaya*). The concepts of bondage and freedom drop away entirely when you realize your fundamental identity is unchanged.

verses.sanskrit:
चित्तं न गच्छति क्वापि न च जीवो विनश्यति ।
आत्मन्येव सदा रमते नित्यतृप्तः सुखी भव ॥
verses.transliteration:
cittaṃ na gacchati kvāpi na ca jīvō vinaśyati |
ātmanyēva sadā ramatē nityatṛptaḥ sukhī bhava ||
verses.english: The mind goes nowhere, and the individual soul never truly perishes. Delighting within your own nature, remain eternally satisfied and happy.
verses.commentary: The mind moves like a pendulum only when fueled by desire. When the egoic driving force drops, the mind rests naturally in the background, showing that consciousness was never broken.
